metrics.narrative: John Deere's core pain is reliance on a homegrown TCS quoting tool and Excel-based bidding, which Karen Powers called a 'nightmare' to customize ('deorizing') during the discovery/demo call on 24 Jun 2025. Zycus proposed metrics including a 40% sourcing cycle-time reduction and 60% enhanced-bid outcomes in the pricing proposal reviewed on 18 Dec 2025, but Karen Powers directly challenged these as 'pretty optimistic' and asked Zycus to reset expectations against John Deere's own environment. Arthur Raguette committed to send a business-case value-engineering sheet, sample output, and a comparable-manufacturing-customer reference call by 5 Jan 2026; no later touchpoint confirms this benchmark package was delivered or that the ROI figures were validated.

sources: Meeting records 2026-01-28, 2026-02-24, 2026-05-11 (not recorded, content per Next Step)
champion.narrative: Karen Pagorek has been the most consistent working-level contact managing the proof-of-concept relationship, exchanging periodic connects with Arthur Raguette (28 Jan 2026, 24 Feb 2026, and most recently 11 May 2026). However, on 11 May 2026 she told Arthur that John Deere is re-assessing priorities and the POC 'will not move forward' for now while she reviews an unidentified alternative I/O provider. This is a champion with real, ongoing access but uncertain current advocacy rather than confirmed strength.

competition.narrative: The Salesforce competitor field lists Oracle and SAP Ariba, but neither is evidenced in any read call. The concrete, evidenced threats are GEP (Arthur Raguette's Next Step names a 'Pilot Spend Analysis to counter GEP'), John Deere's own homegrown TCS build supplemented by OpenAI and Ultrametrics partnerships (the do-nothing/in-house alternative described since 24 Jun 2025), and a newly surfaced, unidentified internal/external I/O provider that Karen Pagorek is evaluating in depth as of 11 May 2026, which directly triggered the POC pause.

identify_pain.narrative: Karen Powers described John Deere's homegrown TCS quoting tool and Excel-based bidding as inefficient and painful to customize ('deorizing... is a nightmare') on 24 Jun 2025, and named specific integration gaps (manual BOM upload versus API). Eduardo Viera separately confirmed on 30 Sep 2025 that 2024 funding was secured against a hard-savings ROI case for automation, showing the pain is budget-backed and organizationally recognized.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, Order Form, or SOW activity has surfaced anywhere in the record. The deal has not progressed past proof-of-concept/pilot conversations and a budgetary pricing proposal reviewed on 18 Dec 2025, which Arthur Raguette himself characterized as 'not anywhere near a final commercial proposal.'

economic_buyer.narrative: Two executive sponsors are named in Salesforce contact roles: Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services) and Joshua Jepsen (SVP & CFO). Eduardo Viera had one confirmed 1:1 with Arthur Raguette on 30 Sep 2025, in which he said John Deere was not pursuing a competitive tender and preferred a blended 'make versus buy' approach, and committed his team to scope a December workshop; he has not appeared in any subsequent deep-read touchpoint, roughly 9 months ago. Joshua Jepsen has never appeared as a participant or been quoted in any read call across the deal's full history.

decision_process.narrative: Per Arthur Raguette's own Next Step notes, the verified path to agreement runs through Karen Pagorek and Eduardo Viera. Eduardo Viera indicated on 30 Sep 2025 that no competitive tender was planned and that his team would define workshop scope, participants and dates for an on-site session; two workshop touchpoints did occur (3 Nov 2025 and 17 Nov 2025) though their content was not captured by the relevant stakeholder that workshop-to-proposal sequence, no formal budget-approval chain or multi-approver sign-off path has been confirmed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Concrete evaluation angles have surfaced in calls: API-based integration with John Deere's product-lifecycle-management tool to auto-import bills of materials (raised by Karen Powers, 24 Jun 2025, confirmed feasible via the iSaaS connector), a clear separation of iSource/e-auction capability from agent-driven autonomous sourcing (requested by Karen Powers, 18 Dec 2025), and should-cost/cost-tracking tied to the existing RFx/RFQ tool. No formal ShoeFit or Business Requirements Document scoring artifact has surfaced in any read source.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 229 (2027-02-22). Deal created 2025-06-06, age 398 days. In Shortlisted stage since unknown date (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c not retrieved). Close date has slipped at least four times per Next_Step__c. Current stage (Shortlisted) requires moving to Vendor Selected within ~60 days to stay on pace for Feb 2027 close, but buyer paused POC in May 2026 to evaluate internal alternative and has not resumed. Last verified activity 2026-07-20, 12 days in the future from today (2026-07-08), indicating scheduled re-engagement call. No two-way buyer engagement since May 2026 call where Karen stated POC 'will not move forward' and they are 're-assessing priorities'. Forward slip risk high: deal has been open 13 months, remains in early-mid stage, and Eduardo (EB) has been unresponsive since ~September 2025.
evidence: Best Case forecast is indefensible: no Economic Buyer engagement in 9 months (Eduardo Viera last contact ~September 2025 per Next_Step), no CFO engagement ever (Joshua Jepsen listed Executive Sponsor but never participated per call history), no commercial or budget approval pathway confirmed, and POC paused May 2026 with buyer exploring internal build or competitive alternative (Next_Step: 'ramifications: on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss'). Champion Karen Pagorek remains engaged but has no budget authority and stated POC 'will not move forward' on 2026-05-11. Deal should be Pipeline until EB re-engages and budget path is locked.

Single-threaded through Karen Pagorek (Global Strategic Sourcing Manager, Influencer role) since May 2026. Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Economic Buyer) and Joshua Jepsen (SVP & CFO, Executive Sponsor) are not engaged. Gia Duke (Director WW Indirect Materials, Evaluator) and Zachary Wenzel (Director Supply Chain, Evaluator) last appeared on calls in December 2025.

POC for ANA and Spend Analytics was delivered March 31, 2026, feedback was 'positive' per Next_Step, but on May 11, 2026 Karen stated they are 're-assessing priorities' and POC 'will not move forward'. Buyer is 'doing a deep dive on another eternal I/O provider' (unidentified, possibly internal build per Next_Step). Arthur reached out to schedule call to understand ramifications; call accepted for July 20, 2026 per Next_Step.

Buyer challenged Zycus's quantified ROI (40% sourcing cycle-time reduction) as 'pretty optimistic' on 2025-12-18 call and requested benchmarks and proof. Arthur committed to deliver benchmarks and reference call by early January 2026. POC delivered March 31 but no confirmation that ROI validation occurred before May pause.
north_star_verdict.headline: POC paused since May 2026 while buyer explores internal I/O alternative; Eduardo Viera (Economic Buyer) silent for 9 months, no budget pathway confirmed, and close date now 229 days out despite multiple slips. Rep-initiated re-engagement scheduled for July 20th has yet to occur; outcome uncertain.

flags: Convert the 2026-07-20 call into a limited-scope proof-of-value pilot (30-day iSource RFX or ANA MRO negotiation) with a specific business outcome (e.g. 3-5% savings on MRO tail spend, or 2-week cycle-time reduction on a strategic sourcing event) — bypass the stalled business-case and benchmarking discussion by proving ROI in 30 days, faster than the internal I/O provider can deliver.

Identify and neutralize the 'eternal I/O provider' threat — Karen stated on 2026-05-11 they are deep-diving another provider (unnamed); Zycus must discover who it is (internal build? partner? competitor?) and position against it with speed-to-value and modular integration wedge on the 2026-07-20 call or the deal will be lost to that alternative.

Economic buyer Eduardo Viera has not engaged since approximately September 2025 (inferred from Next_Step 'need a Jolt to action'); no direct relationship, no budget approval pathway mapped (source: SF Next_Step__c multiple dates, Avoma call history 2025-06 through 2025-12 shows no Eduardo attendance).

CFO Joshua Jepsen, the named Executive Sponsor contact role, has never appeared on a single call in the deal's 13-month history; if the internal evaluation reaches budget sign-off, Zycus has no established relationship at that gate.

Single-thread risk: Karen Pagorek is sole active contact as of May-July 2026; all other stakeholders (Gia Duke, Zachary Wenzel, Karen Powers) last engaged December 2025 or earlier (source: Avoma call history, SF Next_Step__c).

Buyer paused POC in May 2026 and is 're-assessing priorities' while evaluating unidentified 'eternal I/O provider'; Next_Step explicitly names 'in-house build or competitive loss' as ramifications (source: SF Next_Step__c 2026-05-11).

Benchmarks and reference call promised January 2026 to validate 40% cycle-time reduction ROI claim remain undelivered as of POC pause May 2026, leaving value case unproven (source: Avoma call 2025-12-18, SF Next_Step__c).

Champion Karen Pagorek is currently cooling while she evaluates an unnamed internal I/O provider; win the deal by arming her with a head-to-head comparison before her 20 Jul reconnect, not by waiting passively for the outcome.

Executive Sponsor Eduardo Viera has engaged only twice in ten months (30 Sep 2025, 12 Jun 2026); a direct executive-to-executive reinforcement now, while he says 'Zycus is one of those' options, is the highest-leverage lever before John Deere's internal decision lands.
Three explicit requirements unaddressed since December 2025: category list, benchmarks, reference call
ROI (40% cycle-time reduction) challenged as 'pretty optimistic' by buyer and not validated against John Deere data
Close date slipped at least four times; deal age 398 days, still in Shortlisted stage, 229 days to close

Close the buyer-raised ROI credibility gap (benchmark/VE sheet + reference call, promised for early Jan 2026, still unconfirmed) before the 20 July 2026 check-in, since the buyer is actively comparing Zycus's paused pilot against an unnamed alternative.
Use the 20 July 2026 check-in to directly surface and address the undisclosed internal I/O alternative rather than letting it remain a black box.

Buyer doubt on execution/differentiation: the ROI case was called 'pretty optimistic' on 18 Dec 2025 and remains unresolved — deliver the promised benchmark/VE sheet and reference call before or at the 20 Jul 2026 reconnect to rebuild credibility.

Competitive unknown: an unidentified external I/O provider caused the POC pause — the 20 Jul 2026 call should be used explicitly to learn what it is and re-differentiate before John Deere reaches a decision.

Re-establish direct executive sponsor access: Eduardo Viera has not been engaged directly since 30 Sep 2025 and CFO Joshua Jepsen has never engaged; use the 20 Jul 2026 connect with Karen Pagorek to ask for a path back to both.

Use the 20 Jul 2026 connect to get a definitive read on the internal/external I/O-provider evaluation and decide whether to re-launch the paused POC or re-enter narrowly through the already-positive ANA/Spend Analysis result.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus over the alternative has been captured since the 11 May 2026 note that the POC is paused pending an unidentified alternative-provider review; the last positive buyer sentiment on record ('really cool,' Karen Powers, 24 Jun 2025) is over a year old.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of several paths John Deere is weighing against its own homegrown TCS platform. The most current and material threat is an unidentified internal or external I/O provider that Karen Pagorek began deep-diving in spring 2026, which directly triggered the pause of the Zycus proof-of-concept. GEP and the homegrown build are longer-standing, named alternatives; Oracle and SAP Ariba appear only in the CRM competitor field with no supporting call evidence.
